nodes.label: Benchmarking 6DOF Outdoor Visual Localization in Changing Conditions

metadata.title: Benchmarking 6DOF Outdoor Visual Localization in Changing Conditions
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Establishes a harder outdoor visual localization benchmark and evaluation protocol for long-term localization.
metadata.abstract: A benchmark for outdoor 6-DoF localization under day-night and changing-condition challenges.
metadata.problem: Evaluate 6-DoF outdoor visual localization under illumination and appearance changes.
metadata.priorGap: Many localization benchmarks were captured under limited conditions and did not sufficiently stress long-term robustness.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Outdoor localization quality is application-dependent, so multiple thresholds reveal whether methods are useful for coarse retrieval, AR alignment, or precise navigation.
datasetLimitations: Benchmarking focuses on one urban environment and does not cover all weather, seasonal, or geographic variation.
limitations: Dataset-centric evaluation can rank methods without explaining which pipeline component caused failure.

nodes.label: Evaluate 6-DoF outdoor visual localization under illumination and appearance changes.
Many localization benchmarks were captured under limited conditions and did not sufficiently stress long-term robustness.
Pose accuracy at multiple translation and rotation thresholds
Aachen Day-Night
Dataset-centric evaluation can rank methods without explaining which pipeline component caused failure.
ACE: Absolute Camera Estimation for Efficient 3D Visual Localization

metadata.title: ACE: Absolute Camera Estimation for Efficient 3D Visual Localization
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Efficient map learning pipeline that reduces training cost while preserving relocalization quality.
metadata.abstract: Fast and compact training for scene-specific camera relocalization.
metadata.problem: Practical scene-specific visual localization with much lower training and update cost.
metadata.priorGap: Prior scene coordinate regression methods achieved good accuracy but were too expensive to retrain or refresh in dynamic deployment settings.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The paper targets deployment efficiency, so pose quality alone is insufficient without measuring map training cost and refresh speed.
datasetLimitations: Benchmark datasets do not fully expose long-term map drift or large-scale city relocalization under seasonal change.
limitations: Scene-specific training remains necessary for each environment.

nodes.label: Practical scene-specific visual localization with much lower training and update cost.
Prior scene coordinate regression methods achieved good accuracy but were too expensive to retrain or refresh in dynamic deployment settings.
Pose accuracy at 5cm / 5deg
Training time
Cambridge Landmarks
7Scenes
Wayspots
Scene-specific training remains necessary for each environment.
D2-Net: A Trainable CNN for Joint Description and Detection of Local Features

metadata.title: D2-Net: A Trainable CNN for Joint Description and Detection of Local Features
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Improves local matching robustness by learning feature detection and description jointly for image matching and localization.
metadata.abstract: Dense CNN features are used for joint local feature detection and description.
metadata.problem: Produce local features that remain matchable under viewpoint and illumination changes.
metadata.priorGap: Separate handcrafted detectors and descriptors could be brittle, and learned descriptors often still depended on fixed keypoint detectors.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Local feature quality matters only if it improves geometric verification and final camera pose, so matching and pose metrics are both required.
datasetLimitations: Patch and localization benchmarks do not fully capture all domain shifts in large-scale mapping pipelines.
limitations: Dense feature extraction can be computationally heavier than lightweight sparse pipelines.

nodes.label: Produce local features that remain matchable under viewpoint and illumination changes.
Separate handcrafted detectors and descriptors could be brittle, and learned descriptors often still depended on fixed keypoint detectors.
Matching score
Pose accuracy at localization thresholds
HPatches
Dense feature extraction can be computationally heavier than lightweight sparse pipelines.
DSAC: Differentiable RANSAC for Camera Localization

metadata.title: DSAC: Differentiable RANSAC for Camera Localization
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Connects learned scene coordinate regression with robust geometric pose estimation through differentiable hypothesis sampling.
metadata.abstract: Differentiable RANSAC enables end-to-end training of camera localization pipelines with hypothesis selection.
metadata.problem: Train a camera localization system end-to-end while retaining RANSAC-style robustness to correspondence outliers.
metadata.priorGap: RANSAC was effective but non-differentiable, blocking direct optimization of the full pose-estimation pipeline.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Robust localization must be evaluated on final camera pose because intermediate correspondence quality does not guarantee a usable pose estimate.
datasetLimitations: Standard relocalization benchmarks may not expose map-update cost or large-scale outdoor deployment constraints.
limitations: Scene-specific coordinate learning and sampling complexity can limit scaling across many environments.

nodes.label: Train a camera localization system end-to-end while retaining RANSAC-style robustness to correspondence outliers.
RANSAC was effective but non-differentiable, blocking direct optimization of the full pose-estimation pipeline.
Median translation and rotation error
Scene-specific coordinate learning and sampling complexity can limit scaling across many environments.
DSAC*: Differentiable RANSAC for Camera Localization

metadata.title: DSAC*: Differentiable RANSAC for Camera Localization
metadata.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
metadata.summary: Neural-guided scene coordinate regression with differentiable RANSAC for robust camera pose estimation.
metadata.abstract: End-to-end camera localization with differentiable hypothesis selection.
metadata.problem: Robust absolute pose estimation in scenes with ambiguous or noisy correspondences.
metadata.priorGap: Classical RANSAC pipelines were not end-to-end trainable and struggled to learn localization-specific scoring behavior.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Absolute camera localization is deployment-oriented, so thresholded pose accuracy and median pose error directly measure recoverable camera pose quality.
datasetLimitations: Indoor and landmark datasets can underrepresent larger viewpoint shifts and seasonal changes.
limitations: Regression quality can still degrade under severe appearance changes.

nodes.label: Robust absolute pose estimation in scenes with ambiguous or noisy correspondences.
Classical RANSAC pipelines were not end-to-end trainable and struggled to learn localization-specific scoring behavior.
Regression quality can still degrade under severe appearance changes.
LoFTR: Detector-Free Local Feature Matching With Transformers

metadata.title: LoFTR: Detector-Free Local Feature Matching With Transformers
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Removes dependence on sparse keypoint detection and improves matching in low-texture or repetitive regions.
metadata.abstract: A detector-free transformer matcher directly establishes dense-to-coarse local correspondences.
metadata.problem: Establish robust image correspondences when keypoint detectors fail or produce too few stable points.
metadata.priorGap: Keypoint-based pipelines could collapse in low-texture scenes because feature detection happened before global matching context was considered.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Detector-free matching is valuable only if stronger correspondences improve geometry, so relative pose and localization metrics measure the intended downstream effect.
datasetLimitations: Pairwise matching datasets can underrepresent retrieval-stage failures and large-scale map maintenance constraints.
limitations: Transformer matching can increase memory and runtime cost for high-resolution or large-scale deployments.

nodes.label: Establish robust image correspondences when keypoint detectors fail or produce too few stable points.
Keypoint-based pipelines could collapse in low-texture scenes because feature detection happened before global matching context was considered.
Relative pose AUC
Visual localization accuracy
ScanNet
MegaDepth
Transformer matching can increase memory and runtime cost for high-resolution or large-scale deployments.
MARePo: Mapping-agnostic Regression for Efficient Pose Estimation

metadata.title: MARePo: Mapping-agnostic Regression for Efficient Pose Estimation
metadata.summary: Decouples some map-specific assumptions to reduce retraining dependence for pose regression.
metadata.abstract: Regression-based localization with stronger generalization across maps and scenes.
metadata.problem: Reduce scene-specific dependence in regression-based localization pipelines.
metadata.priorGap: Efficient localization models such as ACE still required per-scene optimization and limited cross-scene reuse.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The contribution claims better transferability, so evaluation must include both pose precision and performance beyond the original training scene.
datasetLimitations: Existing benchmarks remain modest in scale compared with open-world city localization.
limitations: Generalization gains may still depend on scene overlap and representation bias.

nodes.label: Reduce scene-specific dependence in regression-based localization pipelines.
Efficient localization models such as ACE still required per-scene optimization and limited cross-scene reuse.
Cross-scene generalization
Generalization gains may still depend on scene overlap and representation bias.
NetVLAD: CNN Architecture for Weakly Supervised Place Recognition

metadata.title: NetVLAD: CNN Architecture for Weakly Supervised Place Recognition
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Provides a retrieval backbone that became a common first stage for large-scale visual localization systems.
metadata.abstract: A trainable VLAD layer learns image retrieval descriptors for place recognition from weak supervision.
metadata.problem: Retrieve visually corresponding places under large viewpoint and appearance changes.
metadata.priorGap: Handcrafted global descriptors and non-trainable aggregation often lacked robustness for city-scale image retrieval.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Retrieval is a candidate-generation stage, so recall at small K directly measures whether the downstream geometric localizer receives useful database images.
datasetLimitations: Place recognition datasets do not fully measure final 6-DoF pose quality without a downstream geometric localization step.
limitations: Retrieval alone cannot estimate metric pose and may fail under severe perceptual aliasing.

nodes.label: Retrieve visually corresponding places under large viewpoint and appearance changes.
Handcrafted global descriptors and non-trainable aggregation often lacked robustness for city-scale image retrieval.
Recall at K
Localization retrieval recall
Pittsburgh 250k
Tokyo 24/7
Retrieval alone cannot estimate metric pose and may fail under severe perceptual aliasing.
Back to the Feature: Learning Robust Camera Localization From Pixels to Pose

metadata.title: Back to the Feature: Learning Robust Camera Localization From Pixels to Pose
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Reintroduces geometric pose refinement around learned features to improve RGB camera localization robustness.
metadata.abstract: Pixel-wise feature alignment refines camera pose through learned dense matching and geometric optimization.
metadata.problem: Refine camera poses from image evidence without depending only on sparse correspondences or direct pose regression.
metadata.priorGap: Retrieval and sparse matching pipelines could produce rough poses, while direct regression often lacked geometric precision.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Pose refinement should be judged by final metric pose precision at strict and relaxed thresholds, not only by feature similarity.
datasetLimitations: Common benchmarks do not fully represent real-time map updates or broad open-world appearance changes.
limitations: Requires a good initialization and can inherit failure modes from retrieval or coarse pose estimation.

nodes.label: Refine camera poses from image evidence without depending only on sparse correspondences or direct pose regression.
Retrieval and sparse matching pipelines could produce rough poses, while direct regression often lacked geometric precision.
Pose accuracy at multiple thresholds
Requires a good initialization and can inherit failure modes from retrieval or coarse pose estimation.
PoseNet: A Convolutional Network for Real-Time 6-DOF Camera Relocalization

metadata.title: PoseNet: A Convolutional Network for Real-Time 6-DOF Camera Relocalization
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision
metadata.summary: Introduces end-to-end learned absolute pose regression as an alternative to handcrafted structure-based localization.
metadata.abstract: A convolutional network directly regresses camera pose from a single RGB image.
metadata.problem: Predict 6-DoF camera pose from a single image in real time for large outdoor scenes.
metadata.priorGap: Structure-based pipelines required explicit feature matching, 3D maps, and robust geometric verification that could be heavy for real-time deployment.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Direct pose regression optimizes continuous camera pose, so median translation and rotation error summarize typical relocalization quality while reducing outlier dominance.
datasetLimitations: Outdoor landmark sequences are limited in geographic diversity and can overrepresent scene-specific appearance.
limitations: Direct regression can be less accurate than geometry-based localization and can struggle with scene generalization.

figure.alt: PoseNet direct camera pose regression schematic
figure.caption: Direct CNN pose regression placeholder

nodes.label: Predict 6-DoF camera pose from a single image in real time for large outdoor scenes.
Structure-based pipelines required explicit feature matching, 3D maps, and robust geometric verification that could be heavy for real-time deployment.
Direct regression can be less accurate than geometry-based localization and can struggle with scene generalization.
Scene Coordinate Regression Forests for Camera Relocalization in RGB-D Images

metadata.title: Scene Coordinate Regression Forests for Camera Relocalization in RGB-D Images
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Establishes scene coordinate regression as a practical camera relocalization formulation using RGB-D inputs.
metadata.abstract: Randomized forests predict scene coordinates from RGB-D image patches for camera relocalization.
metadata.problem: Recover camera pose in a known indoor scene from RGB-D observations without relying on sparse hand-crafted matches.
metadata.priorGap: Classical feature matching and structure-based localization could be brittle for texture-poor indoor RGB-D scenes.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Scene relocalization needs both thresholded success rates and pose-error statistics because downstream AR or robotics use cases fail sharply beyond small pose tolerances.
datasetLimitations: Indoor RGB-D benchmarks underrepresent outdoor scale, long-term appearance change, and low-overlap image retrieval cases.
limitations: Requires scene-specific training and RGB-D style scene supervision.

figure.alt: Scene coordinate regression forest pipeline schematic
figure.caption: Scene coordinate regression forest pipeline placeholder

nodes.label: Recover camera pose in a known indoor scene from RGB-D observations without relying on sparse hand-crafted matches.
Classical feature matching and structure-based localization could be brittle for texture-poor indoor RGB-D scenes.
Requires scene-specific training and RGB-D style scene supervision.
SuperGlue: Learning Feature Matching With Graph Neural Networks

metadata.title: SuperGlue: Learning Feature Matching With Graph Neural Networks
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Makes local feature matching more robust by reasoning over keypoint relationships before geometric verification.
metadata.abstract: A graph neural network performs context-aware feature matching between local features.
metadata.problem: Match local features reliably when descriptors alone are ambiguous or outlier-prone.
metadata.priorGap: Nearest-neighbor descriptor matching ignored global assignment structure and could produce many inconsistent correspondences.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The matcher must improve both correspondence correctness and downstream camera pose estimation, so assignment and pose metrics are both necessary.
datasetLimitations: Matching benchmarks may favor pairwise correspondence quality while full localization also depends on retrieval and mapping quality.
limitations: Graph matching accuracy comes with additional inference cost and dependency on upstream keypoint quality.

figure.caption: Graph neural local feature matcher placeholder

nodes.label: Match local features reliably when descriptors alone are ambiguous or outlier-prone.
Nearest-neighbor descriptor matching ignored global assignment structure and could produce many inconsistent correspondences.
